task_completion.description: The screenshot displays an axial MRI slice with a clearly visible hyperintense (bright) brain tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An axial view of a brain MRI showing a distinct bright tumor region.

task_completion.description: A measurement line (ruler/markup) is drawn across the tumor region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line markup overlay crossing the tumor mass.

task_completion.description: A text annotation is present near the measurement displaying the diameter value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text label visible in the slice view reading 'Max diameter: ...' or similar numeric value in mm.

task_completion.description: The measurement line correctly spans the maximum transverse diameter (widest point) of the tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line connects the furthest edges of the bright tumor region, representing the widest extent.

task_completion.description: The image is a valid screenshot generated from the 3D Slicer environment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image shows the 3D Slicer slice view content or UI elements consistent with the software.

integrity.description: Visual elements match the 3D Slicer interface style.
integrity.visual_evidence: Markup lines, text fonts, and slice view rendering match standard 3D Slicer appearance.

integrity.description: The annotation appears to be created within the software, not added via external image editing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Text and lines are rendered in the 3D/slice space, not overlaid as flat 2D text typical of Paint/Photoshop.

integrity.description: The output is a GUI screenshot, not a terminal text output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Image shows graphical medical data, not command line text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The image displays a BraTS 2021 FLAIR MRI scan. The tumor appears as a hyperintense (bright) region in the brain. The view must be Axial. The measurement line must span the widest part of this bright region to represent the maximum diameter.
